goal: Resolve the autoreload decision path with evidence instead of a write+reload approval loop.
decision: runtime-first: the app disables autoreload at runtime (no file, no reload, verified, reversible), saves once, and can restore the flag afterward; repeated write+reload loops are rejected by design
evidenceChain: 1. runtime set of misc:disable_autoreload proven with exact revert (getoption-verified)
2. with the runtime flag true, collect_autoreload_evidence returns genuine true and the pilot's gates pass
3. the fifteen-gate active-config pilot ran through its designed path: wrote one inert record, verified, restored original bytes (SHA-256 identical), verified
4. reload avoidance proven by marker: the runtime flag stayed true through the config write; a reload would have reset it to the config's false
5. runtime flag restored to the user's original false afterward
userFacing: the Safe Live Save Mode card shows the live state, the blocker, and one-click runtime enable/disable; the one-time-reload confirmation flow is unnecessary because runtime-first avoids the reload entirely
nextRecommendedWork: Generalize save: with Safe Live Save Mode active, allow gated one-shot persistence of previewed values (scalar saves already work via the existing apply flow; structured-family saves go through the now-passing pilot path).
productionOutcome: the runtime-first flow is now production behavior: every Save path verifies Safe Live Save Mode live before writing; the gated family save wrote and verified real config records in its flow proof with zero reloads
